title: tabebuia × del-riscoi
intro: Tabebuia × del-riscoi is a hybrid plant species, resulting from the cross between Tabebuia heterophylla and Tabebuia crenatifolia. It is also known as the "Pau d'arco" or "Ipe rojo" plant, and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant contains medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Tabebuia × del-riscoi include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of various diseases, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules.
